meta.importedFrom: Unit 2 Passage 2 From Pain to Gain（互动课文教学动画）英语新教材外研版八年级下册.html

article.title: From Pain to Gain
article.summary: 从疼痛到收获，理解坚持训练之后的成长和回报。

sentences.text: 21 October 2021
sentences.d: 日记的标准日期格式。

sentences.text: Dear diary,
sentences.tr: 亲爱的日记，
sentences.d: 日记的常用开头格式。

sentences.text: I had a really bad day.
sentences.tr: 我今天过得很糟糕。
sentences.d: 'had a bad day' 是常用搭配，表示过得不顺心。

sentences.text: I tried out for the basketball team, but the coach said I wasn't tall enough.
sentences.tr: 我参加了篮球队的选拔，但教练说我不够高。
sentences.d: 'try out for' 意为参加选拔。'said' 后面省略了 that 引导宾语从句。

sentences.text: This has been my dream since I was a little girl!
sentences.tr: 这是我从小女孩时期开始的梦想！
sentences.g: 现在完成时 + 时间状语从句
sentences.d: 'has been' 表示状态从过去持续到现在。'since' 引导时间状语从句。

sentences.text: I have practised so much, and it all ended today.
sentences.tr: 我练习了这么多，而这一切都在今天结束了。
sentences.d: 前半句用现在完成时强调过去的努力对现在的影响。

sentences.text: I feel very bad...
sentences.tr: 我感觉非常糟糕...
sentences.d: 'feel' 是系动词，后接形容词 'bad'。

sentences.text: 17 September 2025
sentences.d: 时间跨度到了四年后。

sentences.text: Dear diary,
sentences.tr: 亲爱的日记，
sentences.d: 日记的常用开头格式。

sentences.text: I was just reading my diary from four years ago.
sentences.tr: 我刚刚在读四年前的日记。
sentences.d: 'was reading' 表示过去某个时刻正在进行的动作。

sentences.text: How time flies!
sentences.tr: 时间过得真快啊！
sentences.d: 'How + 主语 + 谓语' 构成的感叹句，感叹时间流逝之快。

sentences.text: Back then, I was so disappointed at being too short and failing to get on the basketball team.
sentences.tr: 那时，我对自己太矮以及没能进入篮球队感到非常失望。
sentences.d: 'at' 后接两个并列的动名词短语 'being...' 和 'failing...'。

sentences.text: I couldn't help laughing at my heartbreaking words then.
sentences.tr: 看着当时那些令人心碎的话，我忍不住笑了起来。
sentences.d: 'couldn't help laughing' 意为忍不住笑。

sentences.text: Basketball was really a big deal for me.
sentences.tr: 篮球对我来说真的是件大事。
sentences.d: 'a big deal' 口语中常指重要的事情。

sentences.text: But things changed soon after that.
sentences.tr: 但在那之后不久，情况发生了变化。
sentences.d: 'things' 指代局势或情况。

sentences.text: I was so hurt by my failure back then, and my friends were all worried.
sentences.tr: 那时的失败让我很受伤，我的朋友们都很担心。
sentences.d: 'was hurt by' 被...伤害。

sentences.text: "Why not try something else, like swimming, or skiing?" suggested my best friend.
sentences.tr: “为什么不试试别的呢，比如游泳或滑雪？”我最好的朋友建议道。
sentences.d: 'Why not do sth' 是提建议的常用句型。

sentences.text: At first, I swam just to take my mind off things.
sentences.tr: 起初，我游泳只是为了转移注意力。
sentences.d: 'take one's mind off things' 意为转移注意力，暂时忘却烦恼。

sentences.text: But soon, I fell completely in love with the sport.
sentences.tr: 但很快，我完全爱上了这项运动。
sentences.d: 'fall in love with' 爱上...。

sentences.text: I've won several competitions now and I've made a lot of friends.
sentences.tr: 现在我已经赢得了几场比赛，还交了很多朋友。
sentences.d: 强调过去的经历对现在的影响（赢了比赛，有了朋友）。

sentences.text: My life has taken a different turn.
sentences.tr: 我的人生发生了转折。
sentences.d: 'take a different turn' 意为发生转变。

sentences.text: It's great to look back and see my pains and gains with fresh eyes.
sentences.tr: 回首往事，用新的眼光审视我的痛苦和收获，这种感觉真好。
sentences.d: 'It' 是形式主语，真正的主语是后面的不定式短语 'to look back...'。

sentences.text: Growing up will never be an easy thing.
sentences.tr: 成长绝非易事。
sentences.d: 'Growing up' 作主语，谓语动词用单数。

sentences.text: But we need to look at challenges the right way.
sentences.tr: 但我们需要以正确的方式看待挑战。
sentences.d: 'the right way' 作方式状语。

sentences.text: Now I understand that failure is a part of life.
sentences.tr: 现在我明白了，失败是生活的一部分。
sentences.d: 'that' 引导 understand 的宾语从句。

sentences.text: It may fall upon us at any stage in our lives.
sentences.tr: 它可能发生在我们人生的任何阶段。
sentences.d: 'fall upon' 意为降临到...身上。

sentences.text: I have experienced many other terrible moments since then.
sentences.tr: 从那以后，我还经历过许多其他糟糕的时刻。
sentences.d: 'since then' 是现在完成时的标志词。

sentences.text: But I have never felt so bad again.
sentences.tr: 但我再也没有感到那么糟糕。
sentences.d: 'never' 表示从未。

sentences.text: I know clearly that failure lasts just a moment.
sentences.tr: 我很清楚，失败只是一时的。
sentences.d: 'lasts' 在这里是动词，意为持续。

sentences.text: When one door closes, another opens.
sentences.tr: 上帝关上一扇门，就会为你打开另一扇窗。
sentences.d: 经典谚语，寓意机会总会有的。

sentences.text: Life is waiting for us with many other surprises.
sentences.tr: 生活正以此许多其他的惊喜等待着我们。
sentences.d: 'wait for' 等待。

sentences.text: Tomorrow is another day.
sentences.tr: 明天又是新的一天。
sentences.d: 出自《乱世佳人》，表示对未来的希望。

readingQuestions.stem: Why did the author feel bad in 2019?
options: A. She lost her diary.
B. She failed to get on the basketball team.
C. She had a fight with her best friend.
D. She was sick.
readingQuestions.answer: B. She failed to get on the basketball team.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章第一部分提到 'I tried out for the basketball team, but the coach said I wasn't tall enough.'，因此是因为落选篮球队而难过。

readingQuestions.stem: What did the author's best friend suggest?
options: A. To try out for the basketball team again.
B. To stop playing sports.
C. To try something else like swimming or skiing.
D. To write a diary.
readingQuestions.answer: C. To try something else like swimming or skiing.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章中朋友建议：'Why not try something else, like swimming, or skiing?'

readingQuestions.stem: How does the author feel about the past failure now (in 2023)?
options: A. She is still very angry.
B. She feels ashamed.
C. She can look at it with fresh eyes and even laugh.
D. She wants to forget it completely.
readingQuestions.answer: C. She can look at it with fresh eyes and even laugh.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文中提到 'I couldn't help laughing at my heartbreaking words then' 和 'It's great to look back... with fresh eyes'。

readingQuestions.stem: What does the sentence 'When one door closes, another opens' mean in this context?
options: A. You should always keep doors open.
B. Failure in one area can lead to success in another.
C. Basketball is better than swimming.
D. Life is full of closed doors.
readingQuestions.answer: B. Failure in one area can lead to success in another.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 这句话寓意失去一个机会往往会带来新的机会，正如作者篮球失败后在游泳中找到了成功。

readingQuestions.stem: What is the main theme of this diary?
options: A. How to play basketball.
B. The importance of keeping a diary.
C. Growth and turning failure into gain.
D. Friendship is the most important thing.
readingQuestions.answer: C. Growth and turning failure into gain.
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章标题 'From pain to gain' 以及内容描述的从失败痛苦到获得新生成长的过程，都指向选项C。

end.usageZh: 常见搭配：end with (以...结束)，end in (以...告终)
end.example: The meeting ended at 5 o'clock. (会议在五点钟结束了。)

fail.usageZh: 常见搭配：fail to do (未能做某事)，fail in (在...方面失败)
fail.example: He failed the math test. (他数学考试没及格。)

suggest.usageZh: 常见搭配：suggest that (暗示...)，suggest doing (建议做某事)
suggest.example: Her smile suggested she was happy. (她的微笑暗示她很开心。)
